articles.title: Scientists revive a powerful antibiotic that superbugs had defeated
articles.summary: Scientists have found a way to revive vancomycin, a powerful antibiotic that some dangerous bacteria have learned to resist. Instead of creating an entirely new drug, researchers paired vancomycin with a small molecule…

articles.title: Fig-leaf enzyme turns tannery waste into high-value collagen
articles.summary: Tanneries generate large quantities of skin trimmings before hides are tanned. These trimmings contain collagen but are commonly treated as low-value waste. An international research team has developed a method that…

articles.title: Natural compound may fight rheumatoid arthritis at its source
articles.summary: A plant-derived compound reduced swelling, inflammation, and joint damage in rats with rheumatoid arthritis. It also helped rebalance immune activity and improve the way certain fats were processed in the body…

articles.title: New molecular map reveals how the flu virus hijacks human cells
articles.summary: Scientists uncovered how influenza A commandeers human cells, including a surprising strategy that dissolves tiny structures in the nucleus and releases proteins the virus may use to reproduce. The detailed molecular…

articles.title: Robotic Servicing Mission Launches with NASA Support

articles.title: China's record-breaking Gravity-1 rocket launches 9 satellites from a ship at sea (video)
articles.summary: Orienspace's Gravity-1, the world's most powerful solid rocket, aced its third-ever mission on Tuesday (July 21), launching nine satellites to orbit from a ship at sea.

articles.title: James Webb Space Telescope discovers the secrets of cosmic 'factories' that filled the early universe with stardust
articles.summary: Using the James Webb Space Telescope, astronomers have discovered the secrets of early galaxies that pumped the infant cosmos full of dust, but by studying a much closer and modern galaxy.

articles.title: Artemis III astronauts will beam 4k video from Orion to Earth using SpaceX Starlink lasers
articles.summary: NASA is partnering with SpaceX to outfit the Artemis III Orion spacecraft with Starlink laser communications terminals to assist with high-definition video transmissions and data-heavy mission downlinks.

articles.title: NASA's Roman Space Telescope could reveal black holes ripping up stars. It's set to launch Aug. 30
articles.summary: NASA's next super telescope, the Nancy Grace Roman Space Telescope, may leave nowhere to hide for black holes that violently murder and devour stars, not even cosmic noon.

articles.title: Orcas filmed ramming one of the world's heaviest fish so hard it explodes, possibly for fun

articles.title: Some dinosaurs could rise like giants until they grew too big

articles.title: Florida researchers bring endangered elkhorn coral ashore to protect them

articles.title: Ancient ferns turned Triassic Europe into a wildfire inferno

articles.title: Earth’s waters are quietly running out of oxygen, scientists warn

articles.title: Antarctica may give the world decades to prepare for rising seas

articles.title: Soil organic carbon increases crop yield but can't help with extreme drought

articles.title: Climate change making Europe drought 'more severe': study
articles.summary: Human-caused climate change has intensified a drought across Europe, with extreme heat rather than low rainfall driving the exceptionally dry conditions, a team of international scientists said Thursday.

articles.title: Ancient mystery on K’gari as world’s largest sand island lakes dried up during rainy era

articles.title: MIT’s new lidar chip could give self-driving cars a wider view
articles.summary: MIT engineers have found a way to give chip-based lidar a wider, clearer view without relying on moving parts. Their design uses differently shaped antennas that can sit close together without scrambling one another’s…

articles.title: An ordinary laptop solved a problem thought to require a quantum computer

articles.title: Would you trust AI for ethical advice?
articles.summary: Most people would not prefer to take ethical advice from a computer, but a new Wharton study shows how attitudes change when users see how good AI guidance can be.

articles.title: How much should you reveal about yourself on your dating profile? Scientists have found the 'sweet spot'

articles.title: Molecular clock transitions tune out the noise in the hunt for new physics
articles.summary: Heavy polar molecules are some of the most sensitive tools physicists have for probing what lies beyond the Standard Model, the theory that describes the particles and forces we know about. But turning that sensitivity…

articles.title: Sunshine can help with some serious health problems, argues a new book
articles.summary: While Europe’s heatwave makes us run for shade, the intriguing In Defense of Sunlight reveals sunlight’s health-giving effects – in moderation, says David Robson
